questions.question: Describe the difference between a directed graph and a digraph. Provide an example to illustrate your answer.
questions.hint: A digraph is a type of directed graph

questions.question: What is the difference between a strongly connected digraph and a weakly connected digraph?
questions.hint: Strongly connected digraphs have a path from every vertex to every other vertex

questions.question: Give an example of a digraph that is neither a directed acyclic graph (DAG) nor a strongly connected digraph. Explain why it does not satisfy the conditions for either type of digraph.
questions.hint: Think about a digraph that has cycles and no path from every vertex to every other vertex
